name: Sphenoid bone
description: The front view of the skull showing multiple facial and cranial bones.
Anteriorly, the sphenoid bone can be seen in the deep part of the orbit.
A gap formed by the lesser and greater wings of the sphenoid bone.
Transmits the oculomotor nerve (CN III), trochlear nerve (CN IV), branches of the ophthalmic nerve (CN V1), abducent nerve (CN VI), and the superior ophthalmic vein.
Transmits the optic nerve and the ophthalmic artery.
